title: Browser Origins, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Fields · Language Lens: Cookie Prefix Samesite
summary: A browser API reflects arbitrary Origin with credentials, mutates state on GET, accepts ambient cookies cross-site, caches preflight incorrectly, and treats CORS as server authorization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo cookie prefix samesite increment in browser request trust and cross-origin policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select host-only scope, __Host or __Secure prefixes, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Partitioned, expiry, and rotation for the intended browser flow.
Use safe-method invariants, Go CrossOriginProtection, Sec-Fetch-Site and Origin policy, tokens when needed, and narrowly reviewed bypasses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Cookie Prefix Samesite · Theory Cookie Prefix Samesite
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6517 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to keep CORS response sharing separate from CSRF mutation authorization; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
